item.title: We say it several times a day.
item.dc:creator: Zinfandella

item.title: So this is actually growing now, important to point out the anti disability agenda of previous horrible dictators
item.description: It really feels like they’re trying to attack a very visual form of disability access as a way to be take rights from the disabled. Also this idiots claim of a “disability lobby”. The only way the ADA got passed was people pulling themselves up the stairs of the capital because there were no ramps. It shocked and upset enough people that they passed the ADA (which is still very loosely enforced). Another also, if captions are fine, then lets turn off the audio and see how accurate that captioning is in a real time emergency situation!
item.dc:creator: sureasyourebornyourenevergoingtoseenounicorn

item.title: someProgrammingHumorForANicheAudience
item.description:
1
2. Who else has had a situation like this?
3. Plant growing from a monitor
4. Proof that the monitor works somehow. Is this Green Energy?
5. Simple yet captivating
6
7. Welcome to our date, lady.
8
9. Every damn time...
10
11. This dude might legit be a PSYCHO
12. Awww, baby... you're my number [1]
13. When you ask a senior dev to do something that would take you a day to code
14. I'm not sure I get this one. They like how his shower was arrange or what?
15. Guys! I have something amazing to share, I'm finally at the phase where I'm literally making the credits for my wacky Stanley Parable inspired game. It's called Do Not Press The Button (Or You'll Delete The Multiverse) [don't ask me how I picked the title, it was the sillies most absurd I can think of]. Of course I do have some amazing people to thank in there, but overall I got a little emotional when seeing the credits at the end of the game, because this means that this incredible two year journey is almost at an end. Soon the game will be done and I can say that I've released it. That's all I want currently : ) Anyway, if you want to help with some encouragement, a wishlist is always well appriecated: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_Or_Youll_Delete_The_Multiverse/
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Yesterday all my troubles seemed so far away.Now it looks as though they're here to stay.Oh, I believe in yesterday.
